title: Where No Fan Has Gone Before

jokes.text: Quiet, you! This court-Martial is now in session. The honorably sexy Zapp Brannigan residing.
jokes.setup: Formal court martial opening with proper legal terminology
jokes.punchline: Zapp calls himself 'honorably sexy' instead of 'honorable' - mixing legal formality with his narcissism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp characterization - his vanity bleeding into official proceedings. Well-integrated character comedy.

jokes.text: Do you understand the charges? One beep for yes, two beeps for no.
jokes.setup: Standard legal question about understanding charges
jokes.punchline: Treating a human defendant like a robot with beep responses
jokes.explanation: Absurd legal procedure that fits Zapp's incompetence. Solid character-driven humor.

jokes.text: Double yes. Guilty! I will now carry out the sentence. Kif, my gun. / Wait! He pled not guilty! / Order! Order in the court! Very well then, Mr. Fry. Please recount the events that led you to be guilty.
jokes.setup: Fry pleads not guilty, Zapp misunderstands
jokes.punchline: Zapp immediately moves to execute, gets corrected, then asks Fry to recount events 'that led you to be guilty' - still assuming guilt
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Zapp's incompetence as a judge. The final line shows he's still convinced of guilt despite the plea.

jokes.text: Everybody hit the deck! Shh! Those words are forbidden. What words? Star Trek? Shh! Shut your gills!
jokes.setup: Someone mentions Star Trek casually
jokes.punchline: Immediate panic response treating it like a bomb went off, escalating to 'shut your gills'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and world-building. 'Shut your gills' is perfectly absurd Futurama alien anatomy humor.

jokes.text: You mean the vast migration of Star Wars fans? No. That was the Star Wars Trek.
jokes.setup: Leela asks about 'Star Trek Wars'
jokes.punchline: Zapp corrects her - that was the 'Star Wars Trek', implying multiple confusingly named conflicts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay creating fictional historical events. The economy is perfect - maximum confusion in minimum words.

jokes.text: From a loose association of nerds with skin problems into a full-Blown religion.
jokes.setup: Star Trek fandom evolved over centuries
jokes.punchline: From nerds with skin problems to full religion - both the stereotype and the extreme escalation
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation joke playing on Trek fan stereotypes, though somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: And so the Trekkies were executed in the manner most befitting virgins.
jokes.setup: World leaders were threatened by Trek movement power
jokes.punchline: Dark implication about virgin sacrifice combined with Trek fan virgin stereotype
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good economy, though relies on stereotype. The historical parallel is clever.

jokes.text: Along with that blooper reel where the door doesn't close all the way.
jokes.setup: Sacred texts dumped on forbidden planet
jokes.punchline: Including the famous Trek blooper reel - treating production mistakes as equally sacred
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-reference to actual Trek production issues. Shows deep knowledge while being funny to general audiences.

jokes.text: Another classic science-Fiction show cancelled before its time.
jokes.setup: Star Trek was forever banned from human memory
jokes.punchline: Calling it 'cancelled before its time' when it had 79 episodes and massive cultural impact
jokes.explanation: Smart ironic commentary on Trek's actual longevity vs the 'cancelled too soon' narrative.

jokes.text: I've never heard of such a brutal and shocking injustice... That I cared so little about.
jokes.setup: Zapp seems moved by the tragic story
jokes.punchline: Reveals complete indifference with perfect timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent structure. Pure Zapp - seeming empathetic then revealing selfishness.

jokes.text: She's all yours, buddy.
jokes.setup: Bender called to testify and asked to take the stand
jokes.punchline: Treats the witness stand like he's handing over a woman to another man
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender anthropomorphizing objects with his crude personality. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: You know, 1966? 79 episodes, about 30 good ones.
jokes.setup: Nimoy claims not to remember Star Trek
jokes.punchline: Fry's backhanded compliment about episode quality while trying to help
jokes.explanation: Great meta-commentary on Trek quality while staying true to Fry's tactless character. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Perhaps you're thinking of my one-Man show... About Vincent van Spock? I mean, van Gogh. Damn!
jokes.setup: Nimoy trying to deflect from Star Trek references
jokes.punchline: Accidentally creates 'Vincent van Spock' then catches himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay showing how Trek has infected Nimoy's psyche. The Freudian slip structure is perfect.

jokes.text: But I just signed a six-Month lease on my apartment. I can't walk away from a commitment like that.
jokes.setup: Emotional farewell as cast leaves Earth
jokes.punchline: Nimoy stays for mundane lease obligations instead of friendship
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax - choosing practical concerns over dramatic gesture. Good misdirection structure.

jokes.text: I'm literally angry with rage!
jokes.setup: Shatner expressing frustration about world turning back on them
jokes.punchline: Redundant emphasis - 'literally angry with rage' is hilariously over-dramatic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shatner parody - the redundant dramatic emphasis is exactly his style of overacting.

jokes.text: But it's set 800 years in the past.
jokes.setup: Fry says Star Trek gives people hope for the future
jokes.punchline: Leela points out the logical flaw - it's actually set in their past
jokes.explanation: Smart sci-fi logic joke. Leela as voice of reason puncturing Fry's romantic speech with facts.

jokes.text: 'Cause it taught me so much. Like how you should accept people, whether they be black, white... Klingon, or even female.
jokes.setup: Fry explaining Trek's important social messages about acceptance
jokes.punchline: Lists 'female' last after aliens, revealing his priorities/prejudices
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing Fry's casual sexism even while praising acceptance. Character-consistent.

jokes.text: Forbidden, schmur-Schmidden. What are they gonna do, write a letter? I'm so scared! / I've lost control of the ship!
jokes.setup: Bender dismisses the danger of forbidden space mockingly
jokes.punchline: Immediately loses control, proving his bravado wrong
jokes.explanation: Classic hubris-comeuppance structure with good timing. Bender's dismissive attitude gets immediate payoff.

jokes.text: Oh, dip! / Dip, indeed.
jokes.setup: Fry's mild exclamation at their crash
jokes.punchline: Nimoy's overly formal agreement with the silly phrase
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective contrast between Fry's casual speech and Nimoy's formal response to it.

jokes.text: I feel like hugging you. Well, I would, except you have no body. And we're both men.
jokes.setup: Shatner wants to express affection for reunion
jokes.punchline: Lists practical obstacle first, then reveals deeper hang-up about male affection
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing masculine hang-ups. The practical then emotional obstacles create nice structure.

jokes.text: We did some musical reunion specials in the 2200s. But the guy who played Scotty had trouble yodeling. Ever since then, Welshie has been a welcome participant in our escapades.
jokes.setup: Explaining how 'Welshie' joined the group
jokes.punchline: Scotty actor was replaced for inability to yodel in musical specials
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd backstory. The idea of Trek musical specials requiring yodeling is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: Ven vee voke up, vee had these wah-Dies. / Now say 'nuclear wessels'! No!
jokes.setup: Chekov explaining their situation with his accent
jokes.punchline: Fry immediately wants him to say the famous Trek quote, Chekov refuses
jokes.explanation: Great meta-reference to famous Trek movie quote. Shows fan behavior and actor fatigue with catchphrases.

jokes.text: Check out these abs. / Yowsa!
jokes.setup: Shatner mentions they never age
jokes.punchline: Shows off his abs, Fry's enthusiastic reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about aging Shatner having perfect abs. More setup for character vanity than strong comedy.

jokes.text: Welshie!
jokes.setup: Energy being demonstrates power by vaporizing someone
jokes.punchline: Everyone's shocked reaction to losing 'Welshie'
jokes.explanation: Simple shock reaction beat. The humor comes from the absurdity of the character name and quick elimination.

jokes.text: This court will now hear some very sensual testimony... From this court's ex-Lover, Turanga Leela. / Go **** yourself.
jokes.setup: Zapp introduces Leela as his ex-lover in court
jokes.punchline: Leela's blunt, hostile response shuts him down immediately
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamic - Zapp's inappropriate behavior met with Leela's no-nonsense response. Great timing.

jokes.text: All right, you gas, what's the deal?
jokes.setup: Confronting powerful energy being who just demonstrated lethal powers
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual, dismissive attitude toward cosmic threat
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender - treating a cosmic entity like an annoying stranger. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: I am Melllvar, Seer of the Tapes! Knower of the Episodes! Tremble before my encyclopedic knowledge of Star Trek!
jokes.setup: Powerful energy being reveals himself
jokes.punchline: His ultimate power is... knowing a lot about Star Trek
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of cosmic villain trope. The grandiose titles for mundane fan knowledge is perfect.

jokes.text: Until time stops. / What? You can't do this!
jokes.setup: Asking how long the convention will last
jokes.punchline: Melllvar's casual 'until time stops' response
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the convention experience. The casual delivery of cosmic duration is great.

jokes.text: Can people who hate Star Trek leave? Good question. No, you have to stay even longer! Oh!
jokes.setup: Amy asks reasonable question about leaving
jokes.punchline: Melllvar pretends to consider it then makes punishment worse
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing the villain's sadistic fan behavior

jokes.text: Sign it to Melllvar. Melllvar has three L's. / I think I've done enough conventions to know how to spell Melllvar.
jokes.setup: Melllvar spelling out his name for autograph
jokes.punchline: Shatner's sarcastic response about knowing how to spell the made-up alien name
jokes.explanation: Good convention humor - the absurdity of being corrected on spelling an alien name by the alien himself.

jokes.text: I'm Slim Shady. Yes, I'm the real Shady. All you other Slim Shadys are just imitating. So won't the real Slim Shady please stand up? Please stand up? Please stand up? How can you do a spoken word version of a rap song?
jokes.setup: Shatner performing Eminem in his spoken word style
jokes.punchline: Fry questioning the fundamental contradiction of spoken word rap
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character integration - Shatner's famous spoken word albums meet modern rap. The logical contradiction is perfect.

jokes.text: For 100 quatloos, who did the captain maroon on Ceti Alpha V? / Khan! / Uh, Khan? Correct!
jokes.setup: Trek trivia contest
jokes.punchline: Fry answers correctly but with uncertainty, Melllvar seems surprised he got it right
jokes.explanation: Decent trivia contest humor. The uncertainty in Fry's correct answer and Melllvar's surprise work well.

jokes.text: You have my fan script? / I meant me! Melllvar's the ultimate fan! / Oh, I was confused because the scoreboard says something different.
jokes.setup: Confusion about who wrote the fan script
jokes.punchline: Fry points out the scoreboard contradicts Melllvar's claim to ultimate fandom
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Melllvar's ego. Fry using evidence against the supposed ultimate fan works well.

jokes.text: Usually on the show, someone would come up with a complicated plan... Then explain it with a simple analogy. / Hmm. If we can re-Route engine power through the primary weapons... And reconfigure them to Melllvar's frequency... That should overload his electro-Quantum structure. / Like putting too much air in a balloon! / Of course! It's so simple!
jokes.setup: Leela describes Trek plot formula
jokes.punchline: Fry immediately follows the formula perfectly, complete with balloon analogy
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on Trek plot devices. The immediate execution of the formula after describing it is perfect structure.

jokes.text: Of course! It's so simple!
jokes.setup: Fry's nonsensical balloon analogy
jokes.punchline: Leela pretending the useless analogy clarified everything
jokes.explanation: Good completion of the technobabble parody

jokes.text: Alas, my ship, whom I love like a woman, is disabled.
jokes.setup: Explaining their ship problems
jokes.punchline: Shatner's romantic relationship with his ship, including proper pronoun usage
jokes.explanation: Good Shatner character work. The grammatical care with 'whom' while expressing ship love is nicely absurd.

jokes.text: Captain, I wope he will welp our vessel. / Wessel!
jokes.setup: Chekov speaking in character with accent
jokes.punchline: Shatner correcting his pronunciation, callback to nuclear vessels
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to the earlier Chekov accent joke. The correction creates continuity with his speech patterns.

jokes.text: When I directed Star Trek IV, I got a magnificent performance... Out of Bill because I respected him. And when I directed Star Trek V, I got a magnificent performance out of me... Because I respected me so much!
jokes.setup: Shatner giving directing advice about respecting actors
jokes.punchline: Reveals his ultimate respect is for himself, explaining his own great performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shatner ego moment. The escalation from respecting others to ultimate self-respect is brilliantly executed.

jokes.text: Okay! I'm done re-ca-foobelling the energy motron, or whatever!
jokes.setup: Technical work on the ship
jokes.punchline: Bender's made-up technobabble showing he doesn't understand/care
jokes.explanation: Great Bender moment showing his laziness and fake technical knowledge

jokes.text: My, what a handsome energy creature you are! I love you. / Hey, you wrote it!
jokes.setup: Shatner delivering awkward fan script line to Melllvar
jokes.punchline: Fry pointing out that Melllvar wrote his own love scene
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about fan fiction self-insertion. The awkwardness of the scene plus Fry's observation works well.

jokes.text: It's not working! He's drawing strength from our weapons! / Like a balloon, and something bad happens!
jokes.setup: Plan failing, need new analogy
jokes.punchline: Fry can't complete the balloon analogy properly under pressure
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Fry breaking down under pressure. The incomplete analogy is perfect character work.

jokes.text: Yeah! In the third season, I kissed Shatner.
jokes.setup: Cast defending their heroic actions
jokes.punchline: Nichols cites the famous interracial kiss as her heroic act
jokes.explanation: Smart reference to Trek's groundbreaking TV moment. Treating the kiss as a heroic act has nice historical weight.

jokes.text: Great list, except you forgot episode 66!
jokes.setup: Melllvar listing episodes with combat to the death
jokes.punchline: Fry one-upping him on Trek knowledge during mortal peril
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - can't resist showing off knowledge even when about to die. Good character priority humor.

jokes.text: Great list, except you forgot episode 66!
jokes.setup: Melllvar lists episodes with combat
jokes.punchline: Fry corrects the ultimate fan's incomplete list
jokes.explanation: Shows Fry's deep fan knowledge competing with villain

jokes.text: Can we get on with this? My foot's getting tired.
jokes.setup: Battle to the death being set up dramatically
jokes.punchline: Leela's mundane complaint about standing around
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic tension. Leela's practical concern about comfort during mortal combat is perfect.

jokes.text: You mean Doohan? / Whoever it was, I did it like this. / Ow! My foot's cold.
jokes.setup: Shatner remembering throwing his shoe at enemy
jokes.punchline: Throws shoe at Nimoy, who complains about cold foot
jokes.explanation: Simple slapstick with mundane complaint. The confusion about Doohan adds character detail.

jokes.text: And we can use this machine gun to shoot them! / Yee-Haa!
jokes.setup: Making primitive weapons from nature
jokes.punchline: Bender finds a machine gun, gets excited about violence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from primitive to modern weapons. Bender's enthusiasm for violence is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Just because I'm of Japanese ancestry, you assume I know karate. Have I ever led you to believe I have studied karate? / Well, no. But you never talk about yourself. / Maybe if you showed a little interest.
jokes.setup: Sulu being asked to use karate
jokes.punchline: Reveals hurt feelings about lack of personal interest from crewmates
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment. Addresses stereotyping while revealing deeper emotional issues. Great escalation from offense to hurt.

jokes.text: Just because I'm of Japanese ancestry, you assume I know karate. Have I ever led you to believe I have studied karate?
jokes.setup: Asked to use karate in the fight
jokes.punchline: Takei calls out racial stereotyping
jokes.explanation: Good social commentary delivered through character moment

jokes.text: There's no right way to hit a woman. Then do it the wrong way. Fine.
jokes.setup: Moral dilemma about fighting women
jokes.punchline: Bender's amoral solution, Fry's reluctant agreement
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good character contrast between moral Fry and amoral Bender

jokes.text: There's no right way to hit a woman. / Then do it the wrong way. / Fine.
jokes.setup: Fry's moral dilemma about fighting Leela
jokes.punchline: Leela's practical solution using his own logic against him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical trap. Leela using Fry's own moral reasoning to get what she wants is perfect character work.

jokes.text: Melllvar! Dinnertime! / But, Mom, I'm playing with my collectibles! / Now!
jokes.setup: Powerful cosmic being in midst of battle
jokes.punchline: Called to dinner by his mother like a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of cosmic villain. The domestic reality cutting through epic battle is brilliantly structured.

jokes.text: He's not a child! He's 34!
jokes.setup: Everyone thinking Melllvar is just a child
jokes.punchline: He's actually a 34-year-old living with his parents
jokes.explanation: Great escalation making the situation more pathetic. Adult fan living with parents is perfectly cringe.

jokes.text: We're too heavy! You guys need to lose some weight, fast!
jokes.setup: Ship struggling to escape
jokes.punchline: Fry tells the elderly actors they need to lose weight
jokes.explanation: Awkward moment playing on age/weight sensitivity

jokes.text: Look, Leonard, we're light enough to keep the tapes! Isn't that great? / I'm living in a gefilte-Fish jar.
jokes.setup: Successfully escaping with the tapes
jokes.punchline: Nimoy's underwhelmed response about his cramped conditions
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy with perfect deadpan delivery. The specific food reference adds cultural flavor.

jokes.text: Yes! In mint condition, and you made me take it out of the package!
jokes.setup: Melllvar pursuing them in spaceship
jokes.punchline: He's upset about removing collectible from packaging
jokes.explanation: Perfect collector mentality humor. Even in cosmic battle, he's worried about collectible value.

jokes.text: Thanks a lot, Takei. Now everybody knows!
jokes.setup: Self-destruct sequence being explained
jokes.punchline: Shatner breaking character to complain about Takei revealing the code
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment about revealing plot devices. The actor break from character is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Not just an actor, but a well-Rounded person... With my own friends and credit cards and keys.
jokes.setup: Defending his post-Trek career
jokes.punchline: Lists basic adult accomplishments as major achievements
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing how basic adult milestones become achievements for actors. The specificity is perfect.

jokes.text: Not just an actor, but a well-rounded person... With my own friends and credit cards and keys.
jokes.setup: Fry praising Koenig's post-Trek success
jokes.punchline: Koenig's pathetically basic list of adult accomplishments
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how low the bar is for 'success'

jokes.text: Well, I guess I could move out of my parent's basement... Maybe get a temp job. Whoa. Whoa! One step at a time.
jokes.setup: Melllvar considering life changes
jokes.punchline: Koenig warning him not to rush into basic adult responsibilities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how basic life goals seem overwhelming

jokes.text: Episode 10: 'Balance of Terror.' / More like episode nine, loser! / In your face! Victory is mine!
jokes.setup: Melllvar making Trek reference
jokes.punchline: Fry corrects him and celebrates Trek knowledge victory
jokes.explanation: Good fan rivalry moment. Fry's disproportionate celebration over minor trivia correction is character-perfect.

jokes.text: A full assortment of rum, both spiced and regular.
jokes.setup: Listing paradise amenities
jokes.punchline: Treating basic rum variety as luxury
jokes.explanation: Modest humor about simple pleasures. The specificity of rum types is mildly amusing but not exceptional.

jokes.text: And all you had to put up with was one really annoying Star Trek fan. / Let's get the hell out of here.
jokes.setup: Fry pointing out it wasn't so bad
jokes.punchline: Shatner's immediate desire to escape
jokes.explanation: Good ending irony. Shatner's quick exit despite the paradise shows his priorities clearly.

tmdb_overview: The crew is pitted against the original Star Trek cast to win the devotion of an energy creature.
